vocab.example: Wearing a classic watch contributes to a professional demeanor, signaling that you value both time and tradition.

vocab.meaning: xét về mặt quy tắc ứng xử
vocab.example: Etiquette-wise, glancing at a watch is far less intrusive than pulling out a glowing smartphone during a formal dinner.

vocab.meaning: kín đáo, không gây phiền nhiễu
vocab.example: A wristwatch is an unobtrusive way to keep track of time during a presentation without distracting the audience.
Some argue that in the age of smartphones, wearing a watch has become a matter of redundancy rather than necessity.

vocab.meaning: sự thành thạo/giỏi giang trong quản lý thời gian
vocab.example: Having a clock literally strapped to your arm can subtly enhance your time-management prowess throughout the day.

vocab.meaning: sự phụ thuộc theo thói quen
vocab.example: My habitual reliance on my watch means I feel somewhat 'naked' and disorganized without it.
Winding a mechanical watch offers a tactile experience that a digital screen simply cannot replicate.

vocab.meaning: làm cho bản thân trở nên khác biệt/nổi bật
vocab.example: In a sea of people checking their phones, you can distinguish oneself by relying on a sophisticated timepiece.
Beyond fashion, the functional utility of a dive watch is paramount for those who work in extreme environments.

vocab.meaning: sự gắn kết/hài hòa về mặt thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: A carefully chosen watch provides aesthetic cohesion to an outfit, tying all the visual elements together.
